bounded_excerpt: Last revision: February 2022 3 Guideline Resource Unit 1.0 Oncology Services Services Description Contact & Referral Information 1.1 Cancer Patient Navigation Services: • Radiation Oncology (RO) • Medical Oncology (MO)The service is available to facilitate timely access to information, support, and cancer services, for patients and families during key transition points along the cancer care continuum in Regional and Community based ambulatory cancer centers. T his service supports clients and their families by: • Providing accurate information for patients so they can make informe d de cisions about their next steps . • Helping patients prepare for tests a nd c ancer treatments by explai ning tes t r esults and treatment choices or by r eferring patients to other resources or services. • Improving coordination of care by working with other healthcar e p roviders . • Helping patients prepare for medical visits by explaining ahead of time what to expect . • Helping patients access financial resources and community supports .Calgary : : 403- 521- 3722 : 403- 521- 3245 E dmonton : : 780- 432- 8732 : 780- 432- 8681 G rand Prairie: : 780- 538- 7588 : 780- 532- 9120 L ethbridge : : 403- 3

bounded_excerpt: Last revision: February 2022 5 Guideline Resource Unit 1.0 Oncology Services Services Description Contact & Referral Information 1.4 Rehabilitation Oncology For further control of symptoms such as pain, fatigue, weakness and range of motion in arms and legs, management of spasticity, equipment recommendations including home equipment, and orthoses/br aces. Rehabilitation may involve longitudinal exercise or observation to help determine what goals or functional level patients will have. Calgary: Physiatry, Occupational Therapy, & Physiotherapy: : 403- 476- 2910 : 403- 698- 8199 (Reception) : 403- 476- 2457 Speech Language Pathology : : 403- 355- 3211 : 403- 944- 1256 : 403- 944- 4436 E dmonton: Occupational Therapy (no referral needed): : 780- 432- 8798 : 780- 432- 8716 (Toll -free: 1 -833- 838- 0202) : 780- 432- 8291 Physiotherapy: : 780- 432- 8716 (Toll -free: 1 -833- 838- 0202) : 780- 432- 8291 Speech Language Pathology (no referral needed: : 780- 432- 8288 (Toll -free: 1 -833- 838- 0202) : 780- 432- 8291 L ethbridge: Physiotherapy only: : 403- 388- 6846 : 403- 320- 0508 Red Deer: Occupational Therapy, Physiotherapy, & Speech Language Pathology: : 403- 406- 1963 : 403- 406- 8448

bounded_excerpt: Last revision: February 2022 7 Guideline Resource Unit 2.0 Palliative Services Services Description Contact & Referral Information 2.3 Palliative Consult Services within the Cancer Centres Patients with complex pain and symptom issues related to their cancer or cancer treatments can be referred b y their oncologist for consult with the local Palliative Care team or provider at the Cancer Centre. Consultants facilitate ongoing symptom management and palliative care by patients' primary health care teams, via in -centre consult and collaboration with community and acute care providers/resources. Consult notes and recommendations will be sent to the referring oncologist and the patient’s Family Physician. Patients must have symptoms caused by cancer or cancer treatment. Consultants do not assume the role of most responsible healthcare provider. *T his service is available to current Cancer Centre patients.Calgary: : 403- 521- 3245 AND notify Triage Coordinator of incoming referral: 403 -521- 3589 E dmonton: : 780- 432- 8350 : 780- 432- 8419 *Note: This service is available to currentTBCC/CCI patients. Patients must have symptoms caused by cancer or cancer treatment. A ll Other Cancer

bounded_excerpt: Last revision: February 2022 8 Guideline Resource Unit 2.0 Palliative Services Services Description Contact & Referral Information 2.5 Hospice Hospice care is a specialized setting that provides 24 hour palliative care to individuals nearing the end- of-life. Patients are attended to by Hospice Physicians. Some general criteria include: • Patient has care needs that cannot be met in their current location of care . • Goals of Care Designation of “C1” or “C2” . • Patient has life -limiting illness with estimated prognosis of 3 months or less. • Patient has extensive evidence o f d isease and a demonstrated rate o f d eteri oration of weeks to days . • The patient and/or designated decisio n ma ker is agreeable to no further aggressive or invasive treatmentintended to cure disease . • Palliative Performance Scale (PPS) score of 40% or les s. E ach local hospice might have additional specific criteria. Please contact the palliative care team for more information. • Referral for admission is by Palliative Consultant only, please refer to Palliative Care Consultant section. • To find hospice serv ices in your Zone, refer to Palliative & End of Life Care F or alternate information in Cal

bounded_excerpt: Last revision: February 2022 9 Guideline Resource Unit 3.0 Community Services Services Description Contact & Referral Information 3.1 Integrated Home Care/Palliative Home Care/Community Care Access In-home care and support (example: nursing services, pers onal care, respite services, wound care, case coordination, home care aides, equipment). *L ethbridge covers Cardston, Crowsnest Pass, Fort MacLeod, Milk River, PincherCreek, Raymond, and Taber areas .For more information, see: • Palliative & End of Life Care (PEOLC), Info for Health Professionals • Alberta Referral Directory (ARD) • There are program -specific inclusion and exclusion criteria. bounded_excerpt: Last revision: February 2022 11 Guideline Resource Unit 3.0 Community Services Services Description Contact & Referral Information 3.4 CCA Patient Navigation Patient Navigators are registered nurses with specialized training in cancer care. They can explain treatment choices and test results, and assist with the physical, practical, and emotional challenges tha t come with a cancer diagnosis. Available at 15 community cancer centres across Alberta. Indigenous Cancer Patient Navigators and Adolescent and Young Adult Patient Navigators also available. For more information, see: • Find a Navigator | Alberta Cancer Foundation Services Description Contact & Referral Information 3.5 Your PCN Your Primary Care Network may have access to counseling, social work and other allied health supports closer to home. For more information, see: • Primary Care Networks Services Description Contact & Referral Information 3.6 Grief & Bereavement Services and Care Bereavement care refers to ways that convey genuine care and compassion when offering support at or near the time of death. Alberta: • AHS Bereavement Care Resources • Inform Alberta and search for ‘bereavement’ C algary: • Grief Support Prog
